# Ranger Class Feats — Player Core (PF2e Remastered), Levels 12–20

Complete reference of all Ranger class feats from Player Core (PC), levels 12–20.

Source: [Archives of Nethys](https://2e.aonprd.com/) — Player Core pg. 161–163 (Spring 2026, 1st Printing)

---

## Level 12 (5 feats)

### Distracting Shot
- **ID:** 4893 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 161
- **Description:** The sheer power of your attacks, or the overwhelming number of them, leaves an enemy flustered. If you critically hit your hunted prey with a ranged weapon, or hit it at least twice on the same turn with a ranged weapon, it's off-guard until the start of your next turn.
- **Leads To:** Greater Distracting Shot

### Double Prey
- **ID:** 4894 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162
- **Description:** You can focus on two foes at once, hunting both of them down. When you use the Hunt Prey action, you can pick two creatures as your prey.
- **Archetype Use:** Bounty Hunter*, Game Hunter* *(These archetypes offer Double Prey at a different level than displayed here.)*
- **Leads To:** Shared Prey

### Second Sting
- **ID:** 4895 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Press, Ranger
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162
- **Archetype Use:** Avenger*
- **Requirements:** You are wielding two melee weapons, each in a different hand.
- **Description:** You read your prey's movements and transform them into openings, so failures with one weapon set up glancing blows with the other. Make a melee Strike with one of the required weapons against your hunted prey. The Strike gains the following failure effect.
  **Failure:** You deal the damage the other required weapon would have dealt on a hit, excluding all damage dice. (This removes dice from weapon runes, spells, and special abilities, not just weapon damage dice.)

### Side by Side (Ranger)
- **ID:** 4896 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162
- **Prerequisites:** an animal companion
- **Description:** You and your animal companion fight in tandem. Whenever you and your animal companion are adjacent to the same foe, you are both flanking that foe with each other, regardless of your actual positions.

### Warden's Focus
- **ID:** 4897 | **Level:** 12
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162
- **Prerequisites:** warden spells
- **Description:** Your connection with your surroundings deepens and expands, allowing you to draw in more of nature's primal power as you focus. If you have spent at least 2 Focus Points since the last time you Refocused, you recover 2 Focus Points when you Refocus instead of 1.

---

## Level 14 (4 feats)

### Sense the Unseen
- **ID:** 4898 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Investigator, Ranger, Rogue
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162 / Player Core 2 pg. 112
- **Trigger:** You fail a check to Seek.
- **Description:** When you look for foes, you can catch even the slightest cues, such as their minute movements or the shifting of air currents on your skin. Even though you failed at the triggering check, you automatically sense undetected creatures in the area where you Sought, making them merely hidden to you.

### Shared Prey
- **ID:** 4899 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162
- **Prerequisites:** Double Prey; Warden's Boon
- **Description:** Hunting as a duo, you and your ally both single out your prey. When you use Hunt Prey and select only one prey, you can grant your Hunt Prey benefits and hunter's edge to an ally in addition to gaining them yourself. The ally retains these benefits until you use Hunt Prey again.
- **Leads To:** Triple Threat

### Stealthy Companion
- **ID:** 4900 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162
- **Prerequisites:** Animal Companion; Camouflage
- **Description:** You've trained your animal companion to blend in to its surroundings. Your animal companion gains the benefit of the Camouflage feat. If your companion is a specialized ambusher, its proficiency rank for Stealth increases to master (or legendary if it was already master).

### Warden's Guidance
- **ID:** 4901 | **Level:** 14
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162
- **Description:** You convey your prey's location to your allies with a series of careful words or gestures. As long as your hunted prey is observed by you, all your allies who roll failures and critical failures when Seeking it get a success instead. Your allies need to be able to see or hear you to gain this benefit. You have to be able to call out or make visible gestures for your allies to get this benefit.

---

## Level 16 (5 feats)

### Greater Distracting Shot
- **ID:** 4902 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162
- **Prerequisites:** Distracting Shot
- **Description:** Even a single missile can throw off your enemy's balance, and more powerful attacks leave it flustered for longer. If you hit your hunted prey with a ranged weapon, it's off-guard until the start of your next turn. If you critically hit your prey or hit it twice on the same turn with a ranged weapon, it's off-guard until the end of your next turn instead.

### Improved Twin Riposte (Ranger)
- **ID:** 4903 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162
- **Prerequisites:** Twin Riposte
- **Description:** At the start of each of your turns, you gain an additional reaction that you can use only to perform a Twin Riposte. You can use Twin Riposte even if you aren't benefiting from Twin Parry (though you must be wielding two melee weapons, one in each hand).

### Legendary Monster Hunter
- **ID:** 4904 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162
- **Prerequisites:** legendary in Nature; Master Monster Hunter
- **Description:** Your knowledge of monsters is so incredible that it reveals glaring flaws in your prey. Your bonus from Monster Hunter (and the bonus from Monster Warden if you have it) increases from +1 to +2 for you and any allies who benefit.

### Specialized Companion (Ranger)
- **ID:** 4905 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 162
- **Archetype Use:** Wylderheart*
- **Prerequisites:** Incredible Companion (Ranger)
- **Description:** Your animal companion continues to grow in power and ability, and it is now cunning enough to become specialized. Your animal companion gains one specialization of your choice.
  **Special:** You can select this feat up to three times. Each time, add a different specialization to your companion.

### Warden's Reload
- **ID:** 4906 | **Level:** 16
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** ⭓ (free action)
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 163
- **Frequency:** once per round
- **Requirements:** Your last action or activity this turn was to cast a warden spell.
- **Description:** Your mastery of magic and weapons like the crossbow allow you to wield both with equal ease. Interact to reload a weapon you are currently wielding.

---

## Level 18 (6 feats)

### Impossible Flurry
- **ID:** 4907 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Flourish, Ranger
- **Action:** ⬽ (3 actions)
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 163
- **Requirements:** You are wielding two melee weapons, each in a different hand.
- **Description:** You forgo precision to attack at an impossible speed. Make three melee Strikes with each of the required weapons. Your first attack with each weapon takes the multiple attack penalty as though you had already made one attack this turn. All of the remaining Strikes take the maximum multiple attack penalty.
- **Leads To:** Accurate Flurry

### Impossible Volley *(Fighter/Ranger Shared)*
- **ID:** 4854 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Fighter, Flourish, Ranger
- **Action:** ⬽ (3 actions)
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 150
- **Archetype Use:** Archer*, Eldritch Archer* *(These archetypes offer Impossible Volley at a different level than displayed here.)*
- **Requirements:** You are wielding a ranged weapon with the volley trait and reload 0.
- **Description:** You fire a volley at all foes in an area. Make one Strike with a –2 penalty against each enemy within a 10-foot-radius burst centered at or beyond your weapon's volley range. Roll the damage only once for all targets. Each attack counts toward your multiple attack penalty, but do not increase your penalty until you have made all your attacks.

### Manifold Edge
- **ID:** 4908 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 163
- **Prerequisites:** hunter's edge; masterful hunter
- **Description:** You've learned to leverage every possible edge. When you use Hunt Prey, you can gain a different hunter's edge benefit instead of the one you selected at 1st level. If you do, you don't gain the additional benefit from masterful hunter.

### Masterful Companion
- **ID:** 4909 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 163
- **Prerequisites:** masterful hunter; Animal Companion
- **Description:** Your animal companion shares your incredible hunting skills, allowing it to take down your shared prey with ease. When you Hunt Prey, your animal companion gains the masterful hunter benefit associated with your hunter's edge, rather than just your original hunter's edge benefit.

### Perfect Shot
- **ID:** 4910 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Flourish, Ranger
- **Action:** ⬽ (3 actions)
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 163
- **Requirements:** You are wielding a loaded ranged weapon with reload 1 or more, and you have not reloaded your weapon since your last turn.
- **Description:** After watching the motions of combat with incredible intensity and precision, you fire at your prey at the perfect moment to deliver maximum pain. Make a ranged Strike with the required weapon against your hunted prey. If you hit, the Strike deals maximum damage. After the Strike, your turn ends.

### Shadow Hunter
- **ID:** 4911 | **Level:** 18
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 163
- **Prerequisites:** Camouflage
- **Description:** You blend in to your surroundings so well that others have trouble telling you apart from the terrain. While in natural terrain, you're always concealed from all foes if you choose to be, except for your hunted prey.

---

## Level 20 (4 feats)

### Legendary Shot
- **ID:** 4912 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 163
- **Prerequisites:** legendary in Perception; Far Shot
- **Description:** You focus on your hunted prey, perceiving angles, air resistance, and every variable that would affect your ranged attack. If you have master proficiency with your ranged weapon, you can ignore the penalty for attacking up to five range increments away when attacking your hunted prey.

### To the Ends of the Earth
- **ID:** 4913 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 163
- **Prerequisites:** legendary in Survival
- **Description:** Your ability to track your prey has surpassed explanation, allowing you to trace your prey's movements and predict its location with ease. When you use Hunt Prey on a creature within 100 feet, you can follow that creature's movements, allowing you to know the creature's exact location no matter how far away it becomes, as long as it remains your prey. You must be legendary in Nature to track your prey's location across teleportation or planar travel. This feat gains the detection and primal traits if you're legendary in Nature.

### Triple Threat
- **ID:** 4914 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 163
- **Prerequisites:** Shared Prey
- **Description:** You can divide your attention three ways when hunting. When you use Hunt Prey, you can designate three creatures as prey, designate two creatures as prey and share the effect with one ally (as Shared Prey), or designate one creature as prey and share the effect with two allies.

### Ultimate Skirmisher
- **ID:** 4915 | **Level:** 20
- **Traits:** Ranger
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 163
- **Prerequisites:** unimpeded journey
- **Description:** You are so skilled at navigating the wild, your movement is completely unaffected by terrain. You ignore the effects of all difficult terrain, greater difficult terrain, and hazardous terrain, and you don't trigger traps and hazards that are triggered by moving into an area (such as trip wires and pressure plates), unless you want to.

---

## Notes on Remastered Changes (Levels 12–20)

### Key Differences from Legacy (CRB/APG) Ranger:

**New Feats (not in Legacy):**
- **Second Sting** (L12) — new dual-weapon Press action for failures against hunted prey
- **Warden's Focus** (L12) — new warden spell focus recovery feat
- **Warden's Reload** (L16) — new free-action reload after casting warden spells
- **Perfect Shot** (L18) — new 3-action maximum-damage ranged Strike

**Renamed/Reworked Feats:**
- **Distracting Shot** moved from L10 → L12
- **Double Prey** moved from L10 → L12
- **Stealthy Companion** moved from L10 → L14
- **Warden's Guidance** moved from L10 → L14

**Feats Shared with Other Classes:**
- **Sense the Unseen** (L14) — shared with Investigator, Rogue
- **Impossible Volley** (L18) — shared with Fighter (listed under Fighter in Player Core pg. 150)

**Legacy Feats NOT in Remastered Player Core (L12–20):**
- Wild Stalker (L12) — removed
- Targeting Shot (L14) — removed
- Blind-Fight (moved to a different feat chain)

### Prerequisite Chain References:
Some feats in this file reference feats from the L1–10 file (ranger-feats-pc.md):
- **Warden's Focus** requires: warden spells (via Initiate Warden, L1)
- **Shared Prey** requires: Double Prey (L12) + Warden's Boon (L8)
- **Stealthy Companion** requires: Animal Companion (L1) + Camouflage (L10)
- **Greater Distracting Shot** requires: Distracting Shot (L12)
- **Improved Twin Riposte (Ranger)** requires: Twin Riposte (L10, Fighter/Ranger shared feat, ID 4831)
- **Legendary Monster Hunter** requires: legendary Nature + Master Monster Hunter (L10)
- **Specialized Companion (Ranger)** requires: Incredible Companion (Ranger) (L10)
- **Manifold Edge** requires: hunter's edge + masterful hunter (class features)
- **Masterful Companion** requires: masterful hunter (class feature) + Animal Companion (L1)
- **Shadow Hunter** requires: Camouflage (L10)
- **Legendary Shot** requires: legendary Perception + Far Shot (L4)
- **To the Ends of the Earth** requires: legendary Survival
- **Triple Threat** requires: Shared Prey (L14)
- **Ultimate Skirmisher** requires: unimpeded journey (class feature)

---

## Summary Table

| Level | # Feats | Feat Names |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 12 | 5 | Distracting Shot, Double Prey, Second Sting, Side by Side (Ranger), Warden's Focus |
| 14 | 4 | Sense the Unseen, Shared Prey, Stealthy Companion, Warden's Guidance |
| 16 | 5 | Greater Distracting Shot, Improved Twin Riposte (Ranger), Legendary Monster Hunter, Specialized Companion (Ranger), Warden's Reload |
| 18 | 6 | Impossible Flurry, Impossible Volley*, Manifold Edge, Masterful Companion, Perfect Shot, Shadow Hunter |
| 20 | 4 | Legendary Shot, To the Ends of the Earth, Triple Threat, Ultimate Skirmisher |
| **Total** | **24** | *(23 Ranger-only + 1 shared Fighter/Ranger)* |

\* *Impossible Volley is a Fighter/Ranger shared feat from Player Core pg. 150.*
